subject: Ecology
title: How Energy Enters Ecosystems
description: One-minute skill: How Energy Enters Ecosystems.

hook.text: Almost all life on Earth runs on a single power source: sunlight captured by plants.

buildup.text: Photosynthesis converts solar energy into glucose—chemical fuel for every food chain.

discovery.text: Deep-sea vents are the exception. Bacteria there harvest energy from volcanic chemicals.

twist.text: Without photosynthesis, Earth's surface would be as lifeless as the moon.

climax.text: Every calorie you eat traces back to a plant or microbe that caught energy first.

punchline.text: All food begins with captured light.

quiz.question: What is the primary energy source for most ecosystems?
